  "findings": [
    "Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) level is 55 ppm, which is extremely hazardous and far exceeds the acceptable entry limit of 10 ppm.",
    "Atmosphere is immediately dangerous to life or health (IDLH) for H2S (IDLH threshold is 100 ppm, but entry is prohibited above PEL/TLV without specialized SCBA and permits)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Deny entry into the confined space immediately.",
    "Keep the space isolated and post 'Do Not Enter' signs.",
    "Initiate mechanical ventilation/purge of the vessel and re-test once venting is complete."
  ],
  "rationale": "The pre-entry atmospheric test shows an H2S concentration of 55 ppm. Under OSHA 1910.146 and standard industrial safety practices, the acceptable entry limit for H2S is typically below 10 ppm (OSHA PEL is 20 ppm ceiling, ACGIH TLV is 1 ppm). A reading of 55 ppm is highly toxic and unsafe for entry.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.146(b) - Hazardous atmosphere definition",
    "OSHA 1910.146(c)(5) - Requirements for entry under alternative procedures",
    "OSHA 1910.1000 Table Z-2 - Toxic and Hazardous Substances (H2S limits)"
  ]
}
